metadata.title: Cross-Domain Consistency Score — output-v2 vs output-v3
metadata.methodology: Weighted scoring across 4 dimensions: factual accuracy, severity consistency, eCTD mapping, and source fidelity. Each topic area where files overlap is scored, with contradictions weighted by severity.

overall_consistency_score.interpretation: The corpus is largely consistent on core regulatory facts but has 2 critical contradictions in bioequivalence (BCS biowaivers) and several high-severity issues in data exclusivity characterization and WHO scope claims that require correction before training.

factual_accuracy.details: 2 critical factual contradictions (BCS biowaivers — CONTRA-001, CONTRA-002), 1 high factual error (EU data exclusivity +1 year — CONTRA-003), and 1 medium omission (FDA RSABE scope — CONTRA-008). 14 overlapping topics verified as factually consistent.

severity_rating_consistency.details: Three different severity scales used across the corpus (3-level qualitative in v2, 3-level impact in v3-Japan, 4-level in v3-EU). One explicit severity discrepancy found (Japan stability: moderate vs LOW). Cross-file severity comparison is unreliable without normalization.
severity_rating_consistency.recommendation: Standardize to 4-level scale: critical/high/medium/low across all files

ectd_module_consistency.details: v2 eCTD module assignments are internally consistent and appear correct where verifiable. v3 files omit eCTD mapping entirely — no incorrect assignments found, but the gap means v3 cannot be validated against v2 for CTD placement. No module assignment contradictions detected between files.

source_document_fidelity.details: Where multiple files reference the same source document, extracted information is generally consistent. Key issues: (1) v2 claims WHO TRS 1010 in scope but misses 2 WHO-specific divergences (CONTRA-005, CONTRA-006); (2) v3 WHO file contains misplaced Japan content (CONTRA-010); (3) Water loss rate ratios partially mis-extracted in v2 (CONTRA-007).

shared_sources_checked.consistency: GOOD — core conditions match; v2 misses WHO-specific additions despite claiming scope
EXCELLENT — 25x AUC ratio and MTD definitions match
EXCELLENT — framework description and Japan emphasis consistent
GOOD — QP, GMP, and orphan provisions consistent; data exclusivity characterization contradicts (CONTRA-003)
EXCELLENT — foreign manufacturer accreditation details consistent across all three
FAIR — core conditions match but v2 scope claim is overstated (misses batch reduction, 6-month submission, Zone IVb water loss ratio)

must_fix_before_training.action: Resolve BCS biowaiver positions for FDA and EMA. Current state: v2 and v3 directly contradict each other on both jurisdictions. Verify against ICH M9 (2019) and current FDA/EMA guidance. Update both files to reflect: FDA accepts Class I + III; EMA accepts Class I + III (under ICH M9 / EMA BE guideline conditions).

Fix v2 jurisdiction-divergences.json DIV-021 to correctly attribute the +1 year to 'significant new indication' rather than 'pediatric extension'. Add note that pediatric reward is a separate 6-month SPC extension.

should_fix_before_training.action: Either add WHO-specific batch and submission duration exceptions to v2/stability-rules.json, or remove 'WHO TRS 1010 Annex 10' from its meta.scope to avoid false scope claims.
should_fix_before_training.effort: LOW — add 2 exception rules or edit scope

should_fix_before_training.action: Verify and correct water loss rate ratios in v2/stability-rules.json against WHO TRS 1010 Annex 10 Table 4. Add missing 2.6 ratio.
Update v2 DIV-003 to clarify FDA RSABE applies to both AUC and Cmax.
should_fix_before_training.effort: LOW — add 'and AUC' to existing text

should_fix_before_training.action: Standardize severity scales across all files to 4-level: critical/high/medium/low.
should_fix_before_training.effort: MEDIUM — requires updating all v2 and v3-Japan severity fields

nice_to_fix.action: Remove Japan-specific content from v3/who-requirements.json who_gmp_unique_requirements section.
Add eCTD module mapping to v3 files for parity with v2.
nice_to_fix.effort: HIGH — requires systematic CTD mapping across all v3 rules

summary.note: The raw consistency percentage (56%) reflects the count of topics with any issue. The weighted score (82.5) accounts for the fact that most contradictions are severity-scale or completeness issues rather than hard factual errors. Only 4 of 25 topics have factual contradictions; the remaining 7 issues are severity inconsistencies, scope violations, and structural gaps.
